name: France

colors.symbolism: one of the traditional colours of Paris; associated with Saint Martin and historic French royal devices
the colour of the French monarchy and of the Bourbons
one of the traditional colours of Paris; associated with Saint Denis and the oriflamme

description: Three equal vertical stripes of blue (hoist), white, and red.
history: The tricolour was created during the French Revolution: on 17 July 1789, three days after the storming of the Bastille, Lafayette is credited with combining the red and blue cockade of Paris with the white of the king to form a tricolour cockade. The vertical-striped flag was officially adopted by the National Convention as the national flag on 15 February 1794, with the design attributed to the painter Jacques-Louis David. It was abandoned during the Bourbon Restoration (1814–1830) in favour of the white royal flag, then restored in 1830 by Louis-Philippe. The design has been used continuously since, except during the Vichy period, and is enshrined in Article 2 of the current (1958) Constitution.

features.symbolism: the tricolore: blue and red are the historic colours of Paris, flanking the white of the Bourbon monarchy — combined by the Marquis de Lafayette in 1789 to symbolise the union of the people and the king during the French Revolution
